``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined Most people in prison with sentences of more than six months become eligible for discretionary parole release when they have served one-third of their judicial maximum sentences. people in prison who earn the full credits generally available for good behavior, prison work, and program participation accrue credits at a rate of 17 days per month. Credits are deducted from maximum terms but do not affect parole eligibility minimums.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were 631 people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up 69% of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Demographics The demographics of people in prison past their parole eligibility year reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Criminal Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: In 2020, 61% of people in prison past their parole eligibility year were in prison for violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses of people in prison past their parole eligibility year reveals a varied landscape, with the majority of people incarcerated for rape or sexual assault (22%) and aggravated or simple assault (17%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Original Sentence Lengths In 2020, among the prison population eligible for parole but not yet released, people with sentences between 2 to 4.9 years constituted the majority, representing 35%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 33%, changing from 3,357 in 2010 to 2,238 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series
## Demographics ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity

``` ::: :::

``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases decreased 44%, dropping from 4,121 in 2010 to 2,293 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2020, 43% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 11% decrease compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Rhode Island. The largest change was for other or unknown offenses, which increased by 178%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity In 2020, people in prison past their parole eligibility year collectively spent a total of 237 years behind bars instead of supervised in the community on parole. This extended incarceration varied across different racial and ethnic groups.

## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
## Time Served ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: Black, non-Hispanic individuals faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 1.2 years. White, non-Hispanic individuals experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 0.9 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For Murder and Non-negligent Manslaughter offenses, Black, non-Hispanic individuals had 4.5 more years on average compared to Hispanic, any race individuals, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
